verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Supertubos After work (16:30 start)
verdict.reason: Supertubos is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide is helping, but the spot still looks serious.

best.explanation: Supertubos looks like a clean small window for Worth checking, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 67 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 50/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort.  Watch: spot surf looks too small; 10-13s at high tide can get too backed up for Supertubos / Molhe Leste.

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a fun-sized window for Fun progression, Low confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 93 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 64/100, and wind is the main risk.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small; 10-13s at high tide is softer but still workable on the reef shelves.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Lagide is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide helps a little, but it is not a perfect match.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

photo.alt: Photo of Meio da Baia surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a fun-sized window for Worth checking, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 99 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 64/100, and wind is the main risk.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide is part of the picture, but not the deciding factor.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

ranked.explanation: Molhe Leste looks like a clean small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 67 kJ, 11 s period, workable consistency 57/100, and good wind at 20 km/h.  A jetty-shaped beach break with punchy rights and lefts; this is best for sheltered power when Supertubos has too much size.  Watch: spot surf looks too small; 10-13s at high tide can get too backed up for Supertubos / Molhe Leste; Molhe Leste looks below your bodyboard fun size.
ranked.shortExplanation: Molhe Leste is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide is helping, but the spot still looks serious.
warnings: 10-13s at high tide can get too backed up for Supertubos / Molhe Leste
